# terrible_netapp_ontap_na_ontap_quotas (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `volume` (String) The name of the volume that the quota resides on.
- `vserver` (String) Name of the vserver to use.

### Optional

- `activate_quota_on_change` (String) Method to use to activate quota on a change. Default value is 'resize' in ZAPI. With REST, Changes to quota rule limits `file_limit`, `disk_limit`, `soft_file_limit`, and `soft_disk_limit` are applied automatically without requiring a quota resize operation.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `disk_limit` (String) The amount of disk space that is reserved for the target. Expects a number followed with B (for bytes), KB, MB, GB, TB. If the unit is not present KB is used by default. Examples - 10MB, 20GB, 1TB, 20B, 10. In REST, if limit is less than 1024 bytes, the value is rounded up to 1024 bytes. use '-' to reset disk limit.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `file_limit` (String) The number of files that the target can have. use '-' to reset file limit.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `perform_user_mapping` (Boolean) Whether quota management will perform user mapping for the user specified in quota-target. User mapping can be specified only for a user quota rule.
- `policy` (String) Name of the quota policy from which the quota rule should be obtained. Only supported with ZAPI. Multiple alternative quota policies (active and backup) are not supported in REST. REST manages the quota rules of the active policy.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `qtree` (String) Name of the qtree for the quota. For user or group rules, it can be the qtree name or "" if no qtree. For tree type rules, this field must be "".
- `quota_target` (String) The quota target of the type specified. users and group takes quota_target value in REST. For default user and group quota rules, the quota_target must be specified as "".
- `set_quota_status` (Boolean) Whether the specified volume should have quota status on or off.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `soft_disk_limit` (String) The amount of disk space the target would have to exceed before a message is logged and an SNMP trap is generated. See `disk_limit` for format description. In REST, if limit is less than 1024 bytes, the value is rounded up to 1024 bytes. use '-' to reset soft disk limit.
- `soft_file_limit` (String) The number of files the target would have to exceed before a message is logged and an SNMP trap is generated. use '-' to reset soft file limit.
- `state` (String) Whether the specified quota should exist or not.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `threshold` (String) The amount of disk space the target would have to exceed before a message is logged. See `disk_limit` for format description. Only supported with ZAPI.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `type` (String) The type of quota rule.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
